title: Genomics
description: The Genomics module extends Parthenon's observational research capabilities into the domain of genomic medicine, enabling integration of genetic variant data with clinical and phenotypic information from OMOP CDM sources. It supports genome-wide association study (GWAS) workflows, PheWAS analyses, polygenic risk score computation, and variant-to-phenotype linkage.
tips: Ensure that genotyping array or sequencing metadata is documented alongside your genomic data source registration so that analysts can assess platform compatibility and imputation quality.
PheWAS analyses in Parthenon use cohort definitions as phenotype proxies — invest in well-validated, high-specificity cohort definitions to reduce phenotype misclassification.
Polygenic risk score computation requires a reference LD panel matched to the ancestry of your study population — confirm panel selection with a statistical geneticist before running.
Genomic analyses involving individual-level data are subject to additional governance controls — review your institution's IRB and data use agreement requirements before initiating.
Use the Data Explorer to assess the overlap between patients with genomic data and those meeting your clinical cohort criteria to estimate analytical sample sizes before full analysis runs.
